2025-12-25.abstract: The vote in Myanmar, widely seen as a sham, is a bid for legitimacy by the military government. It is also a way for Beijing to exert its influence there.

2025-12-25.title: ​North Korea Unveils the Completed Hull of What It Calls a Nuclear Submarine
2025-12-25.abstract: The debut followed the North’s first test of a new surface-to-air missile and the arrival of a U.S. nuclear-powered attack sub for a port call in South Korea.

2025-12-25.title: Pope Leo Surprises St. Peter’s Crowd Before Christmas Eve Mass
2025-12-25.abstract: Pope Leo XIV greeted the soggy faithful in St. Peter’s Square in both English and Italian and apologized that there wasn’t enough room in the basilica for them all.

2025-12-26.title: 5 Key Moments in the Rise of India’s Hindu-First Powerhouse
2025-12-26.abstract: The Rashtriya Swayamsevak Sangh, known as the R.S.S., has survived bans and vilification to emerge as the force reshaping India’s secular republic into a Hindu nation.

2025-12-26.title: From the Shadows to Power: How the Hindu Right Reshaped India
2025-12-26.abstract: The far-right group known as the R.S.S., whose members include Prime Minister Narendra Modi, has spent a century trying to make India a Hindu-first nation.

2025-12-26.title: Myanmar’s Civil War Pushes Infectious Disease Over Its Borders
2025-12-26.abstract: Fighting has caused the spread of illnesses like malaria and cholera. In a worst-case scenario, the situation could threaten regional health security, experts say.

2025-12-26.title: From an Olympic Snowboarder to an Accused Drug Kingpin and Killer
2025-12-26.abstract: Ryan Wedding rose to fame as a Canadian Olympic athlete, but the authorities say he became one of the world’s biggest drug lords, who ordered an informant executed.

2025-12-26.title: One Gazan Girl’s Fight to Survive Extreme Hunger
2025-12-26.abstract: After Israel sealed Gaza’s borders, Hoda Abu al-Naja, 12, who suffered from celiac disease, spent months seeking the food and care she needed to combat malnutrition.

2025-12-27.title: Annette Dionne, Last of the Celebrated Quintuplets, Dies at 91
2025-12-27.abstract: She was the first to crawl, the first to cut a tooth, the first to recognize her name, and the last to die. And, like her sisters, she resented being exploited as part of a global sensation.

2025-12-27.title: What Went Wrong Before Hong Kong’s Apartment Inferno
2025-12-27.abstract: Records show how government departments played down residents’ warnings about corrupt practices and substandard materials that fueled the deadly blaze.

2025-12-27.title: Thailand and Cambodia Reach Cease-Fire in Brutal Border War
2025-12-27.abstract: The 72-hour cease-fire could pave the way for an end to the fighting, which has killed dozens and displaced thousands over nearly three weeks.

2025-12-27.title: Before This Physicist Studied the Stars, He Was One
2025-12-27.abstract: Brian Cox once toured as a keyboardist in major rock and pop bands. Now he’s a particle physicist on a new world tour with a dazzling show he designed in an era of science disinformation and denial.

2025-12-27.title: Russia Pummels Kyiv Before Trump-Zelensky Meeting
2025-12-27.abstract: President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine said that the assault with hundreds of drones and missiles, lasting nearly 10 hours, showed that Moscow was not serious about peace.

2025-12-28.title: In Myanmar’s Election, ‘Voting Out of Fear, Not Hope’
2025-12-28.abstract: The voting for Parliament is almost sure to favor the ruling military junta, which is stage-managing the polls. Still, some see them as the most pragmatic way to try to improve conditions.

2025-12-28.title: Families Demand Answers a Year After Deadliest Plane Crash in South Korea
2025-12-28.abstract: Many details of the Jeju Air disaster that killed 179 people remain unclear despite multiple investigations by officials and protests by the victims’ families.

2025-12-28.title: In Guinea Elections, Ruling Junta Seeks Legitimacy While Barring Rivals
2025-12-28.abstract: Whoever wins faces the daunting task of feeding the West African country’s poor and navigating the interests of global powers seeking critical minerals, analysts say.

2025-12-28.title: The Lure of a Rising Asian Metropolis? No Traffic.
2025-12-28.abstract: Indonesia is building a new, green city in the jungle. Its future is far from certain, but new residents like living there.

2025-12-28.title: Trump Calls Petro a ‘Drug Leader.’ What’s the Colombian Leader’s Record?
2025-12-28.abstract: President Gustavo Petro is locked in a war of words with President Trump over Colombia’s major role in the global drug trade. The issue is complicated.

2025-12-29.title: Train Derailment Kills 13 in Mexico
2025-12-29.abstract: The train was carrying around 250 passengers and crew members on a cross-country route linking the Pacific and the Gulf of Mexico. Nearly 100 people were injured.

2025-12-29.title: With Critical Decisions Ahead, Netanyahu Faces Mounting Pressure
2025-12-29.abstract: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel has a series of vexing choices to make in the year ahead on issues including Gaza, conscription and a judicial overhaul, with elections looming.

2025-12-29.title: The World Wants More Ube. Philippine Farmers Are Struggling to Keep Up.
2025-12-29.abstract: Soaring demand and extreme weather worsened by climate change have wiped out harvests of the popular purple yam.

2025-12-29.title: China Mobilizes Forces on Land, Air and Sea for Live-Fire Drill Near Taiwan
2025-12-29.abstract: The exercises ended months of relative calm across the Taiwan Strait, and came after the Trump administration announced plans for arms sales to the island.

2025-12-29.title: Experts Question Denmark’s Vaccine Program as a Model for the U.S.
2025-12-29.abstract: The United States is expected to adopt the vaccine schedule used by Denmark, a much smaller country with universal health care.

2025-12-30.title: Khaleda Zia, Bangladesh’s First Female Prime Minister, Dies
2025-12-30.abstract: In office for three terms, she traded the country’s leadership with Sheikh Hasina, the head of another political dynasty, over decades. She was believed to be 80.

2025-12-30.title: The Tiny Japanese Island on the Front Lines of China’s Feud
2025-12-30.abstract: Yonaguni, a tiny Japanese island near Taiwan, is getting soldiers, radar and missiles. As China’s dispute with Tokyo escalates, some residents are worried.

2025-12-30.title: In Lagos, December Is Time to Get Down and ‘Detty’
2025-12-30.abstract: Detty December means a month of “back to back to back” partying in Nigeria’s megacity, both for locals and visiting members of the diaspora. Bring your stamina, dancing shoes and wads of cash.

2025-12-30.title: China Fires Rockets Near Taiwan in Display of Military Power
2025-12-30.abstract: China’s military also sent warships and aircraft during a second day of exercises designed to show its ability to claim the democratic island.

2025-12-30.title: Emirates to Pull Troops Out of Yemen After Saudi-Led Airstrike
2025-12-30.abstract: Saudi-led forces struck an Emirati shipment early Tuesday, worsening tensions between the once-close allies. The Saudis said the shipment had arms for a separatist group, which the Emiratis denied.

2025-12-31.title: Slow Pace of Flood Recovery Stirs Anger in Far-Flung Corner of Indonesia
2025-12-31.abstract: Protests have erupted in Aceh Province on the northern tip of Sumatra island, where anger and frustration are mounting over what many see as inadequate relief efforts by a distracted government.

2025-12-31.title: Brigitte Bardot’s Legacy of Racist Rhetoric
2025-12-31.abstract: The actress, who died this week at 91, was an icon of 1960s cinema. She was also a hero to the French far right.

2025-12-31.title: Thailand Releases 18 Cambodian P.O.W.s
2025-12-31.abstract: The repatriation of the Cambodian soldiers captured in July comes on the heels of a 72-hour cease-fire that could end months of border skirmishes.

2025-12-31.title: How Russia’s War Machine Brutalizes and Exploits Its Own Soldiers
2025-12-31.abstract: Confidential complaints filed by troops and their families reveal patterns of wrongdoing in the ranks that are hidden from the Russian public.

2025-12-31.title: How Thousands of Secret Russian Documents Were Exposed
2025-12-31.abstract: Russian journalists learned that a government office inadvertently made thousands of sensitive complaints viewable online, including accounts of abuse and coercion in the military.
